GET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/details
Public
Session detail for the checkout iframe.

GET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/connect-params
Public
Challenge for EIP-191 signature.

POST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/connect-external
Public
Bind external wallet via EIP-191 signature.

POST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/pay
Public
Server-signed pay path (Sardis-managed wallet).

POST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/confirm-external-payment
Public
Client-signed USDC transfer; record tx hash.

GET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/balance
Public
Live balance read for the funded wallet.

GET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/stream
Public
SSE stream for status transitions.

POST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/onramp-token
Public
CDP Onramp URL bound to payer wallet.

POST /merchants/{id}/links · GET /merchants/{id}/links
Merchant principal
Reusable checkout links (merchant_checkout_links).

POST /stripe-connect/... (3 routes)
Merchant principal
Onboarding + payout target for USD settlement.

GET/PUT /checkout-controls/config
Merchant principal
Velocity / country / amount / KYC controls.

Source of truth

## Files to grep when something looks off.

File
Why it matters

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/merchant_checkout.py
All /sessions/... endpoints: create, connect, pay, confirm, stream, onramp.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/checkout.py
Legacy /checkout surface used by older integrations; kept for backwards compat.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/merchants.py
Merchant CRUD + checkout links + settlements lookup.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/checkout_controls.py
Merchant-side controls + incident log.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/stripe_connect.py
connected merchant bank onboarding for USD payout.

packages/sardis-api/migrations/031_checkout_hardening.sql
client_secret · idempotency · embed_origin · checkout_links · webhook_deliveries.

packages/sardis-api/migrations/033_wallet_verification.sql
payer_wallet_address column + external_wallet payment method.

packages/sardis-api/migrations/045_merchant_trust.sql
merchant_trust_profiles scoring table.

packages/sardis-checkout/src/sardis_checkout/
Orchestrator · sessions · webhooks · settlement · fraud · idempotency · partial payments.

packages/sardis-checkout-ui/src/
React/Vite checkout UI · CheckoutPage.tsx · embed/sardis-checkout.ts.

packages/sardis-connect/src/sardis_connect/
Python merchant SDK · models · middleware.

packages/sardis-connect-js/src/
TypeScript merchant SDK · middleware.ts · types.ts.

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

12 · Product

Compliance Stack

KYC (Didit) + AML (Elliptic) — what runs before a checkout session is allowed to settle.

13 · Product

Protocol Gateway

x402 / MPP / AP2 — the alternate "front doors" Sardis Connect accepts.

10 · Flows

Settlement & Chains

How USDC lands, how CCTP crosses chains, how payout hits connected merchant bank.

07 · Flows

Payment Flow

The non-checkout agent payment path — same orchestrator, different front door.


=====================================
# COMPLIANCE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/compliance
=====================================

Canvas 12 · Product

## Compliance Stack

The SDK is the distribution. Compliance is the moat. No fintech partner, no regulated merchant,
and no enterprise buyer will let an AI agent move real money through them without seeing a
credible KYC + AML + sanctions + travel-rule + SAR stack behind the API. Sardis ships one.

TL;DR
Six pillars: KYC for humans (Didit), KYB for organizations,
KYA for agents, AML + sanctions for addresses and counterparties,
Travel Rule for cross-VASP transfers above $3K, and SAR for
regulator-bound suspicious activity reports. All of it runs inside the
PreExecutionPipeline on the fail-closed side of the gate.

Why this is the moat

## Anyone can write a payment SDK. Almost nobody ships compliance.

Every payments-for-agents startup demo looks the same on day one: a wallet, a signed
transaction, a receipt. The hard part is day 90 — when a regulated merchant wants to onboard,
the bank wants a BSA report, the auditor asks for retention policy, and the OFAC list just
got a new entry. These aren't optional; they're the line between a demo and a business.

What partners ask for

Stripe Connect onboarding · Circle business account · Bridge originator · Striga banking —
every one of these started with the same question: "show us your KYC / AML / SAR
program and your retention policy". Without those, there is no bank rail, no payout,
no card issuing. Sardis exists as a platform because the compliance stack exists first.

Why the SDK doesn't replace it

SDKs are distribution — they get Sardis into an agent. They don't move money, don't hold
KYC records, and don't satisfy a regulator. The compliance layer is what makes the SDK's
output actually spendable. You can't fork your way around FinCEN 5-year retention.

The six pillars

## What actually runs.

Each pillar is a concrete module in packages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance/
with its own provider abstraction, its own persistence, and its own tests.

Pillar 1 · KYC

## Know Your Customer — for human users.

Primary provider: Didit (YC W26, $0.33/check, 500 free/mo). Document +
liveness + face match. Persona and Coinbase verifications sit behind the same interface.
Module: sardis_compliance/kyc.py + provider providers/didit.py.
Persistence: kyc_verifications (fixed to TEXT agent_id + kyc_status
on users in migration 090).

Pillar 2 · KYB

## Know Your Business — for organizations.

Required before an organization can deploy agents that move money above threshold. Module:
sardis_compliance/kyb.py. Table: kyb_verifications (migration
010) — inquiry_id, provider, status, verified_at, expires_at, business_name. Triggered at
organization onboarding, rechecked on anniversary.

Pillar 3 · KYA

## Know Your Agent — the Sardis original.

Human KYC and business KYB don't cover the thing actually signing: the agent. KYA captures
agent identity (Ed25519/ECDSA-P256 keys), attestation, heartbeat, and state transitions
(register · check · upgrade · suspend · reactivate · stale sweep). Module:
sardis_compliance/kya.py. Ties to agit_policy_engine at decision
time.

Pillar 4 · AML + Sanctions

## OFAC, PEP, adverse media, on-chain.

Providers behind one interface: OFAC, OpenSanctions, Chainalysis, Scorechain, Watchman,
Elliptic (planned). Screens addresses (on-chain counterparty), entities
(PEP / sanctions lists), and transactions (combined). Module:
sanctions.py · pep.py · adverse_media.py. Blocked
addresses cached with TTL.

Pillar 5 · Travel Rule

## FATF R.16 — originator + beneficiary data.

Migration 012 defines travel_rule_transfers: transfer_id, amount, originator
and beneficiary JSONB, protocol (e.g., Notabene). Triggered above $3K USD / $1K EUR.
Module: travel_rule.py + providers/notabene.py. Without this,
VASP-to-VASP transfers are not legally clean.

Pillar 6 · SAR

## Suspicious Activity Reports — FinCEN 5-year retention.

suspicious_activity_reports (migration 011) — sar_id, internal_reference,
activity_type, priority, subject, filing_deadline, report_data. Auto-drafted from anomaly
scoring; filed by humans. Module: sar.py. Five-year retention is a hard legal
constraint; the schema is designed for it.

KYC · Didit flow

## Signup to sk_live_ key in four steps.

Router: packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/kyc_onboarding.py. Webhook
delivery is where a dashboard user becomes a fully provisioned Sardis tenant.

01

Initiate. POST /v1/onboarding/kyc/initiate creates a Didit
session (document + liveness). Returns a hosted URL the dashboard redirects to.

02

User verifies. On Didit's hosted UI. No PII crosses Sardis at this step.

03

Webhook. POST /v1/onboarding/kyc/webhook — Didit posts the
decision with x-api-key auth. Row inserted into kyc_verifications;
users.kyc_status flipped to verified / denied.

04

Live key issued. On verified, the user becomes eligible for
a production API key (sk_live_...). Before that, only sk_test_
sandbox keys are issued. Retry endpoint: POST /v1/onboarding/kyc/retry.

At execution time

## What actually runs inside the Pre-Execution Pipeline.

The orchestrator calls the pipeline once. The pipeline runs the compliance checks in the
order below. Any fail-closed reject is audited with a reason code and surfaced via webhook.

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant O as Orchestrator
participant P as PreExecutionPipeline
participant KYC as KYC (Didit)
participant KYA as KYA (agent)
participant S as Sanctions (OFAC / Chainalysis)
participant F as FIDES (trust graph)
participant TR as Travel Rule (Notabene)
participant SAR as SAR drafter
participant L as Ledger

O->>P: execute(intent)
P->>KYC: user.kyc_status == verified?
KYC-->>P: ok / denied
P->>KYA: agent registered + active?
KYA-->>P: ok / stale / suspended
P->>S: screen(payee, payer, amount)
S-->>P: clear / match / soft-match
P->>F: trust_score(agent_did) ≥ threshold?
F-->>P: tier + signals
alt amount > $3K
P->>TR: originator/beneficiary ready?
TR-->>P: drafted / blocked
end
alt any anomaly signal
P->>SAR: draft report
SAR-->>P: queued for human filing
end
P->>L: log decision (approved | denied)
P-->>O: verdict

[/diagram]

Providers

## Which provider covers which pillar.

Every provider sits behind an interface in
packages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance/providers/. Swaps are
cost-of-one-file changes (Persona → Didit cost one PR).

Pillar
Primary
Pluggable alternates
Status

KYC (humans)
Didit
Persona, Coinbase Verifications, Privado ID
Live

KYB (businesses)
Persona
Circle Compliance, MiCA-specific flow
Live

Sanctions (entities)
OpenSanctions
OFAC (direct), Watchman, Marble
Live

Sanctions (on-chain)
Chainalysis
Scorechain, Elliptic (planned)
Live (Chainalysis); Elliptic planned

PEP / adverse media
OpenSanctions
Marble
Live

Travel Rule
Notabene
—
Schema + drafts live; VASP routing staged

Identity / DID
FIDES
Privado ID
Live (migration 055)

SAR authoring
Sardis native
—
Drafts auto-generated; filing is human.

Agent-native additions

## FIDES + KYA — what human compliance frameworks can't cover.

KYC/AML/Travel Rule were designed for humans and companies. Autonomous agents break both
axes — they don't have passports and they aren't LLCs. Sardis fills the gap with two
modules.

FIDES — decentralized trust graph

Migration 055 adds did_mappings, trust_score_snapshots, and
agents.fides_did / agents.agit_repo_hash columns. An agent's
DID is verified via signature; its trust score is recomputed from on-chain behavior,
incident history, and cross-platform attestations. Router:
routers/fides_identity.py. This is Efe's other open-standards project,
embedded as a first-class compliance signal.

KYA — agent lifecycle

Eleven endpoints under /v1/kya/*: register, get, check, upgrade, suspend,
reactivate, heartbeat, stale-sweep. Module: sardis_compliance/kya.py.
agit_policy_engine.py consumes KYA state at decision time — a suspended or
heartbeat-stale agent fails closed before the transaction reaches the wallet.

Data governance + enterprise controls

## Retention, export, SSO, and per-role RBAC.

Compliance doesn't end when the check passes. Regulators want retention schedules, GDPR
wants export + deletion, and enterprise buyers want SSO before they write a PO.

Concern
Where it lives
What it does

PII classification

Migration 042 · data_classification

Per-column PII type + retention days + anonymize-on-expiry. Seeds: users.email (730d, anonymize), wallets.address (1095d), access_audit_log.ip (90d, anonymize), payer_wallet_address (365d, anonymize).

Retention jobs

Migration 042 · data_retention_log

Every purge run records rows purged, rows anonymized, duration. Operators can prove retention, not just promise it.

GDPR export / deletion

Router data_export.py · compliance_export.py

Per-subject export + delete endpoints. Export bundles are Merkle-anchored so users get tamper-evident copies.

SAR 5-year retention

Migration 011 · suspicious_activity_reports

FinCEN hard requirement. Schema designed for long-horizon archival, not just working-set queries.

SSO (SAML / OIDC)

Migration 043 · sso_config

Per-tenant SSO configuration — required for regulated enterprise buyers.

Advanced RBAC

Migration 044 · advanced_rbac

Role/permission matrix beyond owner/admin/viewer — needed for segregation of duties in banking-adjacent orgs.

Identity registry

Migration 011 · identity_records

TAP/AP2 attestation keys with versioning + revocation — the audit trail that says "this agent claimed this key at this time."

Certification program

## SOC 2 + PCI + ISO + GDPR + AIUC-1.

External certifications that regulated buyers ask for, scoped to a one-year evidence
collection program with a dedicated Trust Control Dashboard running alongside Sardis.

Framework
Scope
Why it matters

SOC 2 Type II
Security, availability, confidentiality
Every US enterprise procurement team asks for this. Without it, no deal.

PCI DSS
Virtual cards flow (Stripe Issuing + planned direct)
Cardholder data environment; required once Sardis Cards GA.

ISO 27001
ISMS — information security management
European + enterprise baseline; pairs with GDPR for EU buyers.

GDPR
EU personal data
Aligns with migration 042 retention + anonymize-on-expiry columns.

AIUC-1
AI agent safety / governance
The new piece. KYA + FIDES + policy decisions map to AIUC-1 controls directly.

Endpoint surface

## What compliance exposes over HTTP.

Endpoint
Module
Purpose

POST /v1/onboarding/kyc/initiate
kyc_onboarding.py
Start Didit session for dashboard user.

POST /v1/onboarding/kyc/webhook
kyc_onboarding.py
Didit decision callback (x-api-key auth).

POST /v1/onboarding/kyc/retry
kyc_onboarding.py
Reattempt after denial or document issue.

POST /v1/compliance/kyc/verify
compliance.py
Generic KYC inquiry (provider-agnostic).

GET /v1/compliance/kyc/{agent_id}
compliance.py
Current KYC status.

POST /v1/compliance/kya/register
compliance.py
Register agent identity + attestation.

POST /v1/compliance/kya/{id}/check · /upgrade · /suspend · /reactivate · /heartbeat
compliance.py
Agent lifecycle state transitions.

POST /v1/compliance/kya/stale/sweep
compliance.py
Bulk expire stale-heartbeat agents.

POST /v1/compliance/sanctions/screen
compliance.py
Screen an entity / address.

POST /v1/compliance/sanctions/screen-transaction
compliance.py
Screen a full transaction (payer + payee + amount).

GET /v1/compliance/sanctions/check/{address}
compliance.py
Cached lookup for a single address.

POST /v1/compliance/sanctions/block
compliance.py
Manual block push (incident response).

GET /v1/compliance/audit/recent · /audit/mandate/{id} · /audit/verify-chain
compliance.py
Queryable compliance audit trail.

GET /v1/compliance/audit/evidence/export
compliance.py
Signed evidence bundle for auditors.

POST /v1/compliance/audit/evidence/verify-signature
compliance.py
Verify a previously exported bundle.

GET /v1/compliance/webhooks/persona/verify
compliance.py
Persona fallback webhook verification.

GET /v1/fides/...
fides_identity.py
DID resolution + trust score queries.

Source of truth

## Where each control actually lives.

File / migration
What it owns

packages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance/
The package. Modules: kyc · kyb · kya · sanctions · pep · adverse_media · travel_rule · sar · risk_scoring · audit_rotation · batch · reports · checks · marble · mica · circle_compliance · coinbase_verifications.

…/providers/
didit · chainalysis · notabene · ofac · opensanctions · privado_id · scorechain · watchman. Behind one interface.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/compliance.py
All /v1/compliance/* endpoints (KYC, KYA, sanctions, audit, evidence).

…/routers/kyc_onboarding.py
Dashboard-facing KYC onboarding (Didit flow).

…/routers/fides_identity.py
FIDES DID + trust score queries.

…/routers/compliance_export.py
GDPR / regulator export bundles.

migrations/010_kyb_verifications.sql
KYB tracking per organization.

migrations/011_sar_and_identity_persistence.sql
SAR (FinCEN 5-yr) + identity_records.

migrations/012_travel_rule.sql
FATF R.16 transfer records.

migrations/042_data_governance.sql
PII classification + retention + anonymize-on-expiry.

migrations/043_sso_config.sql
Per-tenant SSO config (SAML / OIDC).

migrations/044_advanced_rbac.sql
Role / permission matrix beyond basic tiers.

migrations/055_fides_integration.sql
did_mappings · trust_score_snapshots · agents.fides_did.

migrations/090_kyc_verifications_fix.sql
Dashboard user ID compatibility + kyc_status on users.

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

08 · Flows

Policy Engine

How compliance verdicts combine with spending policy at the gate.

19 · Ops

Security Model

Trust boundaries, fail-closed defaults, threat model.

11 · Product

Checkout & Merchants

Where KYC gates payout and merchant trust drives velocity.

17 · Ops

External Services

Every third-party integration backing these providers.


=====================================
# PROTOCOLS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/protocols
=====================================

Canvas 13 · Product

## Multi-Protocol Gateway

Agent payments aren't converging on one standard yet. Google, Visa, Mastercard, Coinbase,
Circle, and Stripe each have their own protocol. Sardis speaks all of them behind a single
orchestrator so a merchant, SDK, or agent doesn't pick a team. One /pay front
door; the right verifier underneath.

TL;DR
Eight protocols wire into the same PaymentOrchestrator:
AP2, TAP, x402, A2A,
ACP, MPP (Stripe/Tempo), ERC-8004,
ERC-8183. Every one of them terminates at the same fail-closed gate,
runs on the same ledger, and emits the same webhook envelope.

The gateway

## One entry, many verifiers.

Callers signal their protocol via route (/v1/ap2/...,
/v1/x402/..., /v1/a2a/...) or via headers. Each protocol has a
dedicated verifier in packages/sardis-protocol/; verified intents feed into
the same orchestrator that wallet-native calls use.

[diagram]

flowchart LR
subgraph Callers
A1[Agent via AP2]
A2[Visa rail · TAP]
A3[API provider · x402]
A4[Google A2A agent]
A5[ACP / Stripe MPP]
A6[On-chain ERC-8183 job]
end

A1 --> R1[ap2.py]
A2 --> R2[tap.py + visa_tap_webhooks.py]
A3 --> R3[x402.py]
A4 --> R4[a2a.py · a2a_payments.py]
A5 --> R5[acp.py · mpp.py]
A6 --> R6[erc8183_policy_guard.py]

R1 --> V[MandateVerifier]
R2 --> V
R3 --> V
R4 --> V
R5 --> V
R6 --> V

V --> G[PreExecutionPipeline
Policy + KYC + Sanctions + Trust]
G --> O[PaymentOrchestrator]
O --> C[ChainExecutor · MPC · Ledger]

[/diagram]

Eight protocols

## Same structure for each.

What it is · who runs it · when to reach for it · where Sardis implements it · the signature shape.

## AP2 — Agent Payment Protocol

consortium

Mandate chain protocol: Intent → Cart → Payment. Each mandate is signed by the
previous actor (user authorizes agent, agent authorizes merchant, merchant authorizes rail).
The verifier walks the whole chain, checking Ed25519 / ECDSA-P256 signatures, TTLs, and
replay cache before the transaction is even scheduled.

Who
Google · PayPal · Mastercard · Visa consortium

Use when
B2B agent commerce · multi-party flows with human approval upstream

Router
routers/ap2.py → /v1/ap2/payments/execute

Verifier
sardis_protocol/verifier.py · mandate_cache.py

POST /v1/ap2/payments/execute
{
"intent_mandate": { "user_did", "agent_did", "scope", "sig" },
"cart_mandate": { "cart", "merchant_did", "sig" },
"payment_mandate": { "amount", "rail", "sig" }
}

## TAP — Trust Anchor Protocol

visa

Visa's agent-identity attestation protocol. mTLS + TRID (Transaction Risk Indicator) for
card-rail transactions. The identity piece lives in
packages/sardis-protocol/src/sardis_protocol/tap.py; webhooks come in via
routers/visa_tap_webhooks.py. Pairs with AP2 when Visa is the rail.

Who
Visa

Use when
Visa card rail is part of the flow (card-present or card-not-present agent purchase)

Router
routers/visa_tap_webhooks.py

Module
sardis_protocol/tap.py · tap_keys.py

agent_id + tap_key (Ed25519)
→ challenge-response attestation
→ TRID score forwarded to Visa auth
→ rail approves / declines

## x402 — HTTP 402 auto-pay

coinbase · circle

Server responds 402 Payment Required with a payment challenge; client
auto-pays in USDC and retries. Revives HTTP's long-dormant 402 as the canonical
pay-per-call path for AI APIs. Sardis implements the verifier, settler, and challenge
generator, with ERC-3009 transfer support.

Who
Coinbase + Circle Gateway facilitators

Use when
API-to-API pay-per-call · AI inference · tool marketplaces

Router
routers/x402.py → /verify · /settle · /challenges · /dry-run

Modules
x402.py · x402_erc3009.py · x402_settlement.py · x402_upto.py

GET /expensive-endpoint
← 402 Payment Required
{ "accepts": [{ "scheme": "exact", "asset": "USDC", "amount": "0.05" }] }
→ sign ERC-3009 transfer → POST /verify
→ receipt attached → retry original call

## A2A — Google Agent-to-Agent

google

Google's open protocol for agents from different platforms to discover each other, trust
each other, and transact. Sardis provides /pay, message envelopes, and a
full trust-table API (peer discovery, relation CRUD, security-policy queries, audit).

Who
Google (open)

Use when
Agents from different platforms transacting

Router
routers/a2a.py + routers/a2a_payments.py

Key endpoints
/pay · /messages · /trust/table · /trust/check · /trust/peers · /trust/relations · /trust/security-policy · /trust/audit/recent

POST /v1/a2a/pay
{ "from_agent_did", "to_agent_did", "amount", "currency", "reason" }
→ trust_check(from_agent, to_agent)
→ mandate issued if trust ≥ threshold
→ settlement via Sardis orchestrator

## ACP — Agent Commerce Protocol

emerging

Emerging standard for agent-mediated commerce — catalog, offer, order, settle.
Sardis ships the router; the protocol is deliberately thin so it can ride Sardis' own
orchestrator + ledger machinery rather than reinventing them.

Who
Emerging · multi-vendor spec

Use when
Catalog-first flows where discovery precedes payment

Router
routers/acp.py

Verbs
offer · order · settle · attestation · payment-proof · dispute

POST /v1/acp/offers — merchant posts
POST /v1/acp/orders — agent orders
POST /v1/acp/settle — orchestrator settles
POST /v1/acp/attest — evidence anchored

## MPP — Merchant Payment Protocol

stripe · tempo

Stripe's protocol layered on Tempo (100K+ TPS EVM L1). Sessions, execute, close, policy
evaluate/simulate, card issuance. Sardis is live on Tempo mainnet (chain_id 4217) via
the dedicated executor in packages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/
and plugs MPP sessions into Sardis policy at mpp_gate.

Who
Stripe + Tempo

Use when
High-throughput merchant flows · card issuance at scale

Router
routers/mpp.py

Endpoints
/sessions · /sessions/{id}/execute · /sessions/{id}/close · /evaluate · /simulate · /cards/issue

POST /v1/mpp/sessions → create
POST /v1/mpp/sessions/{id}/execute → pay
POST /v1/mpp/evaluate → policy decision only
POST /v1/mpp/cards/issue → issue a card

## ERC-8004 — Agent attestation

erc

On-chain attestation standard for agent identity. Provides verifiable claims about
agent code hash, operator, and capabilities. Sardis implements the reader/writer at
packages/sardis-core/src/.../erc8004.py; it feeds into KYA and FIDES
signals consumed at the gate.

Who
ERC standards track

Use when
Regulated deployments needing on-chain attestation of agent code + operator

Module
sardis_v2_core/erc8004.py

Pairs with
KYA lifecycle · FIDES DIDs · AGIT repo hash

agent register → on-chain attestation
{ agent_did, code_hash, operator_addr, sig }
reader verifies → feeds KYA + FIDES signals

## ERC-8183 — On-chain policy + reputation

erc

On-chain policy module + reputation registry + job market for agents. Three modules:
erc8183_policy_guard.py (enforces policy at chain edge),
erc8183_reputation.py (tracks post-trade reputation),
erc8183_job.py (job lifecycle). Launched with conservative caps (1% fee,
USDC-only, trust hooks disabled) — audit-driven relaxation path.

Who
ERC standards track · Sardis-authored implementation

Use when
On-chain agent jobs · cross-platform reputation portability

Modules
erc8183_policy_guard.py · erc8183_reputation.py · erc8183_job.py

Caps
≤1% fee · USDC-only · trust hooks disabled pre-audit

Job posted → policy_guard evaluates
→ agent accepts → work → settle
→ reputation registry updates score

Decision matrix

## When to reach for which protocol.

Most real deployments use two at once (e.g., AP2 for the mandate chain, x402 for the actual
API call). The matrix below is the first-cut decision tree.

Use case
Primary
Secondary
Tradeoff

AI agent buys an API call
x402
—
Lowest friction · assumes crypto rail · USDC only

Agent books travel / multi-party purchase
AP2
TAP (if Visa)
Heaviest verification · strongest audit · slowest

Two agents on different platforms pay each other
A2A
AP2
Built-in trust graph · requires peer A2A support

Merchant wants Stripe-native billing
MPP
x402
Stripe-familiar · needs Tempo access

Card-rail purchase from agent
TAP + AP2
—
Visa-native · requires mTLS + Visa onboarding

On-chain job for an agent
ERC-8183
ERC-8004
Fully on-chain · permissionless · 1% cap until audit

Need verifiable agent identity
ERC-8004
FIDES (migration 055)
Anchored · interoperable · needs operator public key

Generic catalog + order flow
ACP
x402 for payment leg
Thin spec · rides Sardis orchestrator

Shared primitives

## What every verifier reuses.

The reason supporting eight protocols is tractable: they all reduce to the same three
problems (signature verification, replay prevention, mandate archival). Sardis builds
those once.

Module
Role

sardis_protocol/verifier.py
Ed25519 / ECDSA-P256 / secp256k1 signature verification core.

sardis_protocol/mandate_cache.py
Canonical mandate store — every protocol's signed payload lands here.

sardis_protocol/replay_cache_redis.py
Redis-backed replay cache. TTL per protocol; required in prod.

sardis_protocol/nonce_registry.py
Per-agent, per-protocol nonce tracking to defeat replay even without Redis.

sardis_protocol/reason_codes.py
Canonical deny-reason catalog shared across protocols and audit.

sardis_protocol/rate_limiter.py
Protocol-aware rate limiter (stricter for public protocols).

sardis_protocol/payment_methods.py
Rail selection — USDC / ERC-3009 / Visa / card / Lightning.

sardis_protocol/fides_signing.py
FIDES DID signing adapter used by AP2 and A2A.

sardis_protocol/eas_kya.py
EAS (Ethereum Attestation Service) bridge for KYA facts.

sardis_protocol/zkpass_transgate.py
zkPass TransGate — zero-knowledge claims surfaced into protocols.

sardis_protocol/paladin_privacy.py
Paladin privacy layer for sensitive mandate fields.

sardis_protocol/kleros.py
Kleros dispute adapter — protocol-level dispute resolution.

sardis_protocol/schemas.py
Cross-protocol Pydantic schemas; one definition per shape.

Endpoint surface

## Every protocol front door.

Protocol
Endpoint
Purpose

AP2
POST /v1/ap2/payments/execute
Verify mandate chain and execute.

TAP
POST /v1/webhooks/visa-tap (via visa_tap_webhooks.py)
Visa TAP event ingestion.

x402
POST /v1/x402/verify
Verify a client-signed 402 payment payload.

x402
POST /v1/x402/settle
Settle a verified 402 payment on-chain.

x402
GET /v1/x402/settlements/{payment_id}
Settlement status lookup.

x402
POST /v1/x402/dry-run
Server-side simulation before signing.

x402
POST /v1/x402/challenges
Issue a 402 challenge (server-side helper).

A2A
POST /v1/a2a/pay
Cross-platform agent payment.

A2A
POST /v1/a2a/messages
Signed inter-agent envelopes.

A2A
GET /v1/a2a/trust/table · /trust/peers · /trust/audit/recent
Trust graph queries.

A2A
POST /v1/a2a/trust/check · /trust/relations · DELETE /trust/relations
Trust relation CRUD + check.

A2A
GET /v1/a2a/trust/security-policy
Published security policy for peers.

ACP
POST /v1/acp/offers · /orders · /settle · /attest
Full ACP commerce loop.

MPP
POST /v1/mpp/sessions · /execute · /close
Stripe/Tempo session lifecycle.

MPP
POST /v1/mpp/evaluate · /simulate
Policy decision without execution.

MPP
POST /v1/mpp/cards/issue
Card issuance via Stripe Issuing bridge.

ERC-8004
on-chain · read/write via SDK
Attestation read/write (not HTTP front door).

ERC-8183
on-chain · Sardis-deployed modules
Policy guard / reputation / job registry.

Source of truth

## Files to grep.

File
Owns

packages/sardis-protocol/src/sardis_protocol/
Verifier core, replay cache, mandate cache, nonce registry, reason codes, schemas, per-protocol helpers.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/ap2.py
AP2 mandate-chain execute endpoint.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/visa_tap_webhooks.py
Visa TAP webhook ingestion.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/x402.py
x402 verify / settle / dry-run / challenges.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/a2a.py
Google A2A payments + trust graph.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/a2a_payments.py
A2A payments helper endpoints.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/acp.py
Agent Commerce Protocol endpoints.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/mpp.py
Stripe MPP / Tempo sessions + policy + card issue.

packages/sardis-api/src/sardis_api/routers/mpp_demo.py
Stripe MPP hackathon demo endpoints.

packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/erc8004.py
ERC-8004 attestation adapter.

packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/erc8183_policy_guard.py
On-chain policy guard at the chain edge.

packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/erc8183_reputation.py
Agent reputation registry.

packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/erc8183_job.py
Agent job market lifecycle.

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

07 · Flows

Payment Flow

How a verified mandate becomes an on-chain transfer.

08 · Flows

Policy Engine

What the gate does once the mandate is verified.

12 · Product

Compliance Stack

KYC / KYA / sanctions that every protocol ultimately feeds.

11 · Product

Checkout & Merchants

The zero-crypto merchant front door that accepts Sardis / x402 / MPP.


=====================================
# SDKS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/sdks
=====================================

Canvas 14 · Product

## SDKs & CLIs

Nine distinct surfaces ship the same Sardis API to wherever the agent actually lives —
a Python script, a Next.js app, a Claude Desktop chat, an n8n workflow, a Telegram
conversation, a checkout page, or a terminal. One auth model (sk_ API keys),
one semantic contract, nine idiomatic entry points.

TL;DR
The SDK layer is intentionally thin: each binding wraps the same REST surface at
api.sardis.sh with types, errors, retries, and pagination. Nothing "clever"
happens client-side — policy, mandate, and MPC signing stay server-authoritative.
The cleverness lives in how many environments the same API can reach without the
developer learning anything new.

Surface Map

## Nine packages, one API.

Every box below is a real directory under packages/. Version and published
artifact columns are pulled from the packages' own manifests.

01 · Python

sardis-sdk

Async-first httpx client. Resources for agents, wallets, mandates, payments, policies, holds, cards, fx, groups.

v1.1.0
PyPI
py ≥3.12

02 · TypeScript

@sardis/sdk

ESM + CJS + browser bundles. Zod types, 30 resource modules, streaming + interceptors + pagination.

v1.1.0
npm
Node ≥18

03 · CLI (Python)

sardis-cli

Click-based. 17 command groups: auth, wallets, agents, payments, policies, mandates, holds, cards, chains, fiat, approvals, groups, spending, ledger, demo, init.

v1.0.0
PyPI

04 · CLI (Go)

sardis (binary)

GoReleaser-built cross-platform binary — darwin/linux/windows × amd64/arm64. Ships with Homebrew tap.

v0.1.0
6 targets
Homebrew

05 · CLI (JS)

@sardis/cli

npm-installable variant for Node-heavy teams who want the CLI inline with JS toolchains.

npm

06 · MCP Server

@sardis/mcp-server

Exposes 50 Sardis tools over Model Context Protocol. Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf.

v1.1.0
50 tools
stdio

07 · n8n Node

n8n-nodes-sardis

Community node + credential. Drop Sardis actions into any n8n workflow.

v1.0.0
n8n ≥2.0

08 · Spend Widget

sardis-spend-widget

Embeddable consumer widget — lets end users approve/deny agent spend in real time from the agent app.

embed

09 · Telegram Bot

sardis-telegram-bot

Telegram front-end for approvals, balance checks, kill-switch. For solo ops + on-call.

Python

Auth

## One API-key model across every surface.

Every surface authenticates with a prefixed secret key. Prefix encodes environment and
blast radius; require_principal in the API gateway hashes the key (SHA-256),
looks up the owning merchant/agent, and attaches a principal to the request for the
orchestrator to consume.

Prefix
Environment
Use
Rotation

sk_live_…
Production
Real money, real compliance, real rate limits.
Hot-rotatable from dashboard; old key decays 24h.

sk_test_…
Sandbox
Policy & mandate logic end-to-end, but settlement is simulated.
Self-serve in dashboard.

sk_dev_…
Local
Emitted by sardis auth login against local API.
Never leaves laptop.

pk_live_…
Public / client
Checkout, spend widget — rate-limited, read-biased scope only.
Safe to embed.

01 · Python SDK

## sardis-sdk — async httpx client.

Lives at packages/sardis-sdk-python/. Async is the default
(AsyncSardisClient); sync exists for scripts. Connection pooling, retry with
exponential backoff, token refresh, and typed errors are built-in.

## Install & quickstart

# install
pip install sardis-sdk
# or
uv add sardis-sdk

# set API key
export SARDIS_API_KEY=sk_live_…

From packages/sardis-sdk-python/examples/quickstart.py — the canonical flow:
create agent → create wallet → parse a natural-language policy → pay → check balance.

import asyncio, os
from sardis_sdk import AsyncSardisClient

async def main():
async with AsyncSardisClient(api_key=os.environ["SARDIS_API_KEY"]) as client:
agent = await client.agents.create(name="My First Agent")
wallet = await client.wallets.create(agent_id=agent.agent_id, chain="base")

policy = await client.policies.parse(
text="Max $100 per transaction, $500 per day, no gambling",
)
await client.policies.apply(wallet_id=wallet.wallet_id, policy=policy)

payment = await client.payments.send(
wallet_id=wallet.wallet_id,
to="merchant_demo",
amount="25.00",
purpose="API usage - OpenAI",
)
print(payment.status, payment.tx_id)

balance = await client.wallets.get_balance(wallet.wallet_id)
print(balance.balance, balance.currency)

asyncio.run(main())

## Resources exposed on AsyncSardisClient

agents
wallets
payments
policies
mandates
holds
cards
fx
groups
approvals
funding
evidence
escrow
exceptions
kill_switch

## Typed error handling

Every error inherits from SardisError — catch the base, or narrow to
specifics. Source: packages/sardis-sdk-python/src/sardis_sdk/models/errors.py.

from sardis_sdk import (
AsyncSardisClient,
AuthenticationError, # 401 — bad or revoked key
RateLimitError, # 429 — quota exceeded (respects Retry-After)
ValidationError, # 400 — malformed request body
NotFoundError, # 404 — unknown wallet / agent / mandate
TimeoutError, # request-level timeout
ConnectionError, # transport failure before response
APIError, # 5xx catchall
)

try:
await client.payments.send(...)
except RateLimitError as e:
await asyncio.sleep(e.retry_after)
except AuthenticationError:
# rotate key and bail
raise
except APIError as e:
log.error("sardis %s: %s", e.status_code, e.code)

02 · TypeScript SDK

## @sardis/sdk — strict TS, Zod-validated.

Lives at packages/sardis-sdk-js/. Ships three bundles from one source —
ESM (dist/index.js), CJS (dist/cjs/index.cjs), and a browser
build (dist/browser/index.js). Strict TypeScript, Zod runtime validation at
boundaries, interceptors, abort signals, and auto-paginators.

npm i @sardis/sdk
# or
pnpm add @sardis/sdk

import { SardisClient, RateLimitError, AuthenticationError } from "@sardis/sdk";

const client = new SardisClient({ apiKey: process.env.SARDIS_API_KEY! });

const agent = await client.agents.create({ name: "My First Agent" });
const wallet = await client.wallets.create({ agent_id: agent.agent_id, chain: "base" });

const policy = await client.policies.parse({
text: "Max $100 per transaction, $500 per day, no gambling",
});
await client.policies.apply({ wallet_id: wallet.wallet_id, policy });

try {
const payment = await client.payments.send({
wallet_id: wallet.wallet_id,
to: "merchant_demo",
amount: "25.00",
purpose: "API usage - OpenAI",
});
console.log(payment.status, payment.tx_id);
} catch (err) {
if (err instanceof RateLimitError) {
await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, err.retryAfter * 1000));
} else if (err instanceof AuthenticationError) {
throw err;
}
}

## Resource modules (src/resources/)

agents
wallets
payments
policies
mandate-delegation
holds
cards
fx
groups
approvals
kill-switch
ledger
evidence
escrow
treasury
subscriptions-v2
checkout
marketplace
streaming
webhooks
a2a
ucp
simulation
usage
batch
transactions
funding
payment-objects

## Auto-pagination

for await (const agent of client.paginate(
(params) => client.agents.list(params),
{ limit: 100 },
)) {
console.log(agent.name);
}

03 · CLI (Python)

## sardis-cli — Click-based terminal tool.

Lives at packages/sardis-cli/. One command group per resource, thin wrapper
around the Python SDK. Config lives in ~/.sardis/config.toml
(commands/auth.py writes it). See commands/ for the full tree.

# install
pip install sardis-cli

# log in — exchanges email/password or API key for a local token
sardis auth login

# scaffold a new project (API keys, .env, example agent)
sardis init my-agent

# list wallets for the current org
sardis wallet list

# create one
sardis wallet create --agent ag_123 --chain base

# set a natural-language policy
sardis policy set --wallet wal_abc \
--text "Max $100 per tx, $500/day, no gambling"

# pay
sardis pay --wallet wal_abc --to merchant_demo --amount 25.00 \
--purpose "OpenAI API usage"

# one-command end-to-end demo (uses sandbox)
sardis demo run

## Command groups

auth
init
agents
wallets
payments
policies
mandates
holds
cards
chains
fiat
approvals
groups
spending
ledger
demo

04 · CLI (Go)

## sardis binary — cross-platform release.

Lives at packages/sardis-cli-go/. Built with GoReleaser; release metadata at
dist/metadata.json. One static binary, six targets, Homebrew tap under
dist/homebrew/.

Target
Artifact

darwin / amd64
sardis_darwin_amd64.tar.gz

darwin / arm64
sardis_darwin_arm64.tar.gz

linux / amd64
sardis_linux_amd64.tar.gz

linux / arm64
sardis_linux_arm64.tar.gz

windows / amd64
sardis_windows_amd64.zip

windows / arm64
sardis_windows_arm64.zip

# Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install sardis/tap/sardis

# Verified checksums in dist/checksums.txt and dist/digests.txt
sardis --version # v0.1.0

05 · CLI (JS)

## @sardis/cli — the npm-native variant.

Lives at packages/sardis-cli-js/. Same command surface as the Python CLI,
distributed via npm so Node-heavy teams can pin it in package.json and keep
their agent toolchain in one registry.

npx @sardis/cli auth login
npx @sardis/cli wallet list
npx @sardis/cli pay --wallet wal_abc --to merchant_demo --amount 25.00

06 · MCP Server

## @sardis/mcp-server — Claude, Cursor, Windsurf.

Lives at packages/sardis-mcp-server/. Implements Model Context Protocol over
stdio; exposes 50 Sardis tools to any MCP-aware client. Tool list is declared in the
package's mcp manifest (package.json) — truth source below.

## Claude Desktop config

From CLAUDE_DESKTOP_CONFIG.md. macOS path:
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json.

{
"mcpServers": {
"sardis": {
"command": "npx",
"args": ["@sardis/mcp-server", "start"],
"env": {
"SARDIS_API_KEY": "your_sardis_api_key",
"SARDIS_WALLET_ID": "wal_YOUR_WALLET"
}
}
}
}

Omit the env block to run in simulated mode — no key needed, no funds move,
useful for demos and first-touch.

## Tools exposed (50)

Grouped by domain. Each one maps to one Sardis REST call.

## Wallets & balance

sardis_get_balance
sardis_get_wallet
sardis_create_wallet
sardis_list_wallets
sardis_fund_wallet
sardis_withdraw
sardis_withdraw_to_bank
sardis_get_funding_status
sardis_get_withdrawal_status
sardis_list_funding_transactions

## Agents & groups

sardis_create_agent
sardis_get_agent
sardis_list_agents
sardis_update_agent
sardis_create_group
sardis_get_group
sardis_list_groups
sardis_add_agent_to_group
sardis_remove_agent_from_group
sardis_get_group_spending

## Payments & holds

sardis_pay
sardis_get_transaction
sardis_list_transactions
sardis_create_hold
sardis_capture_hold
sardis_void_hold
sardis_release_hold
sardis_extend_hold
sardis_get_hold
sardis_list_holds

## Policy & compliance

sardis_check_policy
sardis_validate_limits
sardis_check_compliance
sardis_get_policies
sardis_request_approval
sardis_get_approval_status
sardis_check_approval
sardis_list_pending_approvals
sardis_cancel_approval

## Cards

sardis_issue_card
sardis_create_card
sardis_get_card
sardis_list_cards
sardis_freeze_card
sardis_unfreeze_card
sardis_cancel_card

## Spending analytics + sandbox

sardis_get_spending_summary
sardis_get_spending
sardis_get_spending_by_vendor
sardis_get_spending_by_category
sardis_get_spending_trends
sardis_sandbox_demo

07 · n8n Node

## n8n-nodes-sardis — community node.

Lives at packages/n8n-nodes-sardis/. Ships one node
(nodes/Sardis/Sardis.node.ts) plus a credential type
(credentials/SardisApi.credentials.ts) that securely stores the API key in
n8n's encrypted credential store. Requires n8n-workflow ≥ 2.0.

# Install inside an n8n instance
npm i -g n8n-nodes-sardis

# Or add via n8n Community Nodes UI: Settings → Community Nodes → Install
# Package: n8n-nodes-sardis

Example flow: Gmail → Sardis: Request Approval → Wait → Sardis: Pay.
The Sardis node exposes operations for pay, hold, approve, balance,
and webhook dispatch so the workflow can stay in one tool.

08 · Spend Widget

## sardis-spend-widget — consumer-side approval UI.

Lives at packages/sardis-spend-widget/. An embeddable widget that consumer
agent apps drop in so the end user — not the agent — confirms spend above a
threshold. Uses a pk_live_… publishable key scoped to a single session;
sensitive operations still go through the server.

<script src="https://widget.sardis.sh/v1/spend.js" async></script>

<sardis-spend
publishable-key="pk_live_…"
session-id="ss_live_abc123"
on-approve="handleApprove"
on-deny="handleDeny">
</sardis-spend>

The widget subscribes over SSE to spend requests for the session and renders a native-feel
approve/deny sheet. Complements the Telegram bot surface for users who live in a browser
rather than in chat.

09 · Telegram Bot

## sardis-telegram-bot — on-call surface.

Lives at packages/sardis-telegram-bot/. Python bot that mirrors the
approval / balance / kill-switch surface into Telegram. Built for solo operators and
on-call rotations who want real-time agent-spend pings in a chat they already have open.

# typical commands (the bot is side-loaded, not published)
/balance # shows wallet balances across chains
/approvals # lists pending approvals, with approve/deny buttons
/kill <wallet> # pauses all outbound spend for a wallet
/status # last 10 ledger decisions

Shape

## Nine entry points, one pipeline.

Whichever surface originates the call, the request hits the same API gateway and
the same PaymentOrchestrator. This is what makes "add a new surface"
cheap — the trust model doesn't change.

[diagram]

flowchart LR
PY["Python SDK"] --> API
TS["TS SDK"] --> API
CLI["sardis-cli (py)"] --> API
GO["sardis (go binary)"] --> API
JCLI["@sardis/cli"] --> API
MCP["@sardis/mcp-server"] --> API
N8N["n8n node"] --> API
WID["spend-widget"] --> API
TG["telegram-bot"] --> API
API["api.sardis.sh · require_principal"] --> O["PaymentOrchestrator"]
O --> PP["Pre-Execution Pipeline"]
PP --> CX["Chain / Wallet / Rails"]

[/diagram]

10 · Release Cadence

## How SDKs actually ship.

Two workflows in .github/workflows/ drive publishing. Tag pushes trigger
production releases; workflow_dispatch supports manual rc builds with a
TestPyPI / npm prerelease toggle. No SDK version leaves CI without green tests.

Workflow
Triggers
Targets
Registries

release-python-sdk.yml

Tag py-sdk-v* or manual dispatch

packages/sardis-sdk-python

PyPI (prod) / TestPyPI (prerelease)

release-python-integrations.yml

Tag py-<integration>-v*

sardis-langchain, sardis-crewai, sardis-autogpt, …

PyPI / TestPyPI

release-npm.yml

Tag mcp-v*, sdk-js-v*, ai-sdk-v*, connect-js-v*

MCP server, TS SDK, AI SDK, Connect JS

npm

sign-artifacts.yml

On release builds

All published artifacts

Sigstore attestations

Source of Truth

## Files to read if the above drifts.

File
Why

packages/sardis-sdk-python/pyproject.toml
Python SDK version, deps, classifiers.

packages/sardis-sdk-python/src/sardis_sdk/client.py
AsyncSardisClient construction, retry, error mapping.

packages/sardis-sdk-python/examples/quickstart.py
The canonical end-to-end flow.

packages/sardis-sdk-js/package.json
TS SDK version, bundle exports.

packages/sardis-sdk-js/src/index.ts
Public surface: client, errors, telemetry, pagination.

packages/sardis-sdk-js/src/resources/
30 resource modules.

packages/sardis-cli/src/sardis_cli/commands/
One file per command group.

packages/sardis-cli-go/dist/metadata.json
GoReleaser build metadata + commit.

packages/sardis-mcp-server/package.json
mcp.tools[] — the real tool list, not this page.

packages/sardis-mcp-server/CLAUDE_DESKTOP_CONFIG.md
OS-specific config paths + prod/simulated snippets.

packages/n8n-nodes-sardis/package.json
n8n node + credential registration.

.github/workflows/release-python-sdk.yml
Python publish logic.

.github/workflows/release-npm.yml
npm publish logic for 4 packages.

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

15 · Product

AI Framework Integrations

The 18 framework adapters that sit on top of these SDKs.

04 · Backend

API Surface

The 123 routers these SDKs call.

16 · Product

Dashboard UI

Where keys are minted, rotated, and scoped.

18 · Ops

CI/CD

The 24 workflows that ship this surface.


=====================================
# INTEGRATIONS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/integrations
=====================================

Canvas 15 · Product

## AI Framework Integrations

Sardis is the universal payment layer for every major agent framework. Not "works with
a few" — every one that matters today ships a Sardis adapter in
packages/, with the same policy engine, the same MPC wallets, and the same
ledger underneath. The developer writes agent code; Sardis makes its money verifiable.

19 published adapters
70K+ SDK installs
LangChain v1.0.0
MCP-first

Positioning
Frameworks come and go; money movement doesn't. By landing in every agent framework's
native idiom, Sardis positions itself as the neutral payments substrate — the same way
Stripe became neutral for web checkout. Developers don't choose "payments" any more than
they choose "HTTPS"; they pick a framework and Sardis is already there.

Shape

## Every adapter is the same three-line pattern.

A framework adapter doesn't re-implement Sardis — it translates. Each package imports
sardis-sdk (or @sardis/sdk), wraps Sardis primitives
(pay, check_balance, set_policy,
list_transactions, check_policy) as native tools in that
framework's tool protocol, and exposes them as a SardisToolkit.
The agent author writes code that looks native; the bytes on the wire are identical
across every framework.

[diagram]

flowchart LR
LC["LangChain agent"] --> AL["sardis-langchain"]
CA["CrewAI crew"] --> AC["sardis-crewai"]
AG["AutoGPT block"] --> AA["sardis-autogpt"]
OA["OpenAI Agents SDK"] --> AO["sardis-openai-agents"]
CS["Claude Agent SDK"] --> AK["sardis-agent-sdk"]
GD["Google ADK"] --> AD["sardis-adk"]
BU["Browser Use agent"] --> AB["sardis-browser-use"]
CK["Coinbase AgentKit"] --> AX["sardis-agentkit"]
VE["Vercel AI SDK"] --> AV["@sardis/ai-sdk"]
NG["n8n / Activepieces"] --> AN["node + piece"]
MCP["MCP host (Claude/Cursor)"] --> AM["@sardis/mcp-server"]

AL --> SDK["sardis-sdk (py) · @sardis/sdk (ts)"]
AC --> SDK
AA --> SDK
AO --> SDK
AK --> SDK
AD --> SDK
AB --> SDK
AX --> SDK
AV --> SDK
AN --> SDK
AM --> SDK

SDK --> API["api.sardis.sh"]
API --> O["PaymentOrchestrator → Pre-Execution Pipeline → MPC → chain"]

[/diagram]

Adding a new framework is a one-day job: wrap the five primitive tools in its tool schema,
ship as a package, add an example. The policy engine, trust graph, and ledger don't change.

Group 01 · General-purpose orchestration

## Where most LLM agents actually live.

Six frameworks. If someone ships an "agent" in 2026, it is almost certainly built on one
of these. Each adapter returns a SardisToolkit whose .get_tools()
plugs directly into that framework's agent loop.

LangChain

sardis-langchain · v1.0.0

SardisToolkit → five BaseTools (Pay, CheckBalance, SetPolicy,
CheckPolicy, ListTransactions) plus a SardisCallbackHandler that logs every
tool invocation to the Sardis ledger. Works with any LangChain agent (OpenAI Functions,
ReAct, tool-calling).

pip install sardis-langchain

from sardis_sdk import SardisClient
from sardis_langchain import SardisToolkit

client = SardisClient(api_key="sk_live_…")
tools = SardisToolkit(client=client, wallet_id="wal_abc").get_tools()
# pass tools to any LangChain agent

Exports: SardisToolkit · SardisCallbackHandler · SardisPayTool · SardisCheckBalanceTool · SardisSetPolicyTool · SardisCheckPolicyTool · SardisListTransactionsTool

CrewAI

sardis-crewai · v1.1.0

Agent payment tools plus pre-built Sardis agents and task templates —
"Finance Officer," "Spending Approver" — that drop into a CrewAI crew.
Multi-agent crews share a wallet with per-agent policy overlays.

pip install sardis-crewai

AutoGPT

sardis-autogpt · v2.0.0

AutoGPT blocks that expose Sardis pay, check_balance, and
policy ops as first-class workflow nodes. 2.x bumped to match AutoGPT's block
architecture refactor.

pip install sardis-autogpt

OpenAI Agents SDK

sardis-openai-agents · v1.1.0

Sardis tools as Tool instances for OpenAI's Agents SDK — drop into
Runner to give any GPT-class model policy-controlled payments.
Complementary to sardis-openai (classic function-calling).

pip install sardis-openai-agents

Claude Agent SDK

sardis-agent-sdk · v1.1.0

Tool definitions + SardisToolHandler for Anthropic's Agent SDK.
Covers pay, hold, escrow, subscription, FX quote, policy, balance,
transaction list, and mint — a wider surface than most adapters because Claude can
handle richer tool schemas.

pip install sardis-agent-sdk

Exports: SardisToolkit, SardisToolHandler, SARDIS_PAY_TOOL, SARDIS_CHECK_BALANCE_TOOL, SARDIS_CREATE_HOLD_TOOL, SARDIS_CREATE_ESCROW_TOOL, SARDIS_CREATE_SUBSCRIPTION_TOOL, SARDIS_GET_FX_QUOTE_TOOL, SARDIS_SET_POLICY_TOOL, SARDIS_MINT_PAYMENT_TOOL, SARDIS_LIST_TRANSACTIONS_TOOL, SARDIS_CHECK_POLICY_TOOL

Google ADK

sardis-adk · v1.0.0

Wraps Sardis ops as FunctionTools for Google's Agent Development Kit.
Ships a create_sardis_agent() helper that returns a pre-configured ADK
agent with wallet + policy wired up.

pip install sardis-adk

Group 02 · Browser & automation

## Agents that click, type, and execute shell.

Browser and code-execution agents have the biggest "oops I drained the wallet" risk —
they touch live websites and run arbitrary code. Every adapter here force-routes spend
through the policy engine before a browser action or shell command can spend money.

Browser Use

sardis-browser-use · v1.1.0

Payment tools for the Browser Use agent. Converts an "agent shops on a real e-commerce
site" flow from "trust the DOM" to "Sardis hold + checkout mandate" — actions are
reversible until the agent explicitly captures.

pip install sardis-browser-use

Stagehand / Browserbase

sardis-stagehand

Stagehand actions with a Sardis payment primitive attached. Agents running in
Browserbase sessions get the same hold/capture/policy model as any other Sardis agent.
TypeScript package.

npm i sardis-stagehand

Composio

sardis-composio · v1.0.0

Sardis tools in Composio's tool marketplace — so any agent already pulling tools from
Composio picks up Sardis without a code change beyond adding the tool name.

pip install sardis-composio

OpenClaw

sardis-openclaw · v1.0.0

OpenClaw skill — makes Sardis payments a native capability for OpenClaw's
computer-use agents. Same five-primitive pattern.

pip install sardis-openclaw

E2B

sardis-e2b

E2B sandbox template that ships Sardis SDK pre-installed and pre-authenticated to the
merchant's sandbox key. Agents that spin up E2B VMs to run code can immediately call
sardis.pay(...) without a key-distribution dance.

e2b template init sardis-e2b

Group 03 · Multi-agent protocols

## When agents talk to each other.

Agent-to-agent payments have a protocol problem before they have a payments problem.
Sardis ships first-class support for the two multi-agent standards that matter:
Google's A2A and Coinbase AgentKit.

Google A2A

sardis-a2a · v1.1.0

Spec-compliant A2A server and client. Agent card discovery at
/.well-known/agent.json, JSON-RPC 2.0 task lifecycle, SSE streaming,
push notifications. Sardis extends with payment-domain skills (escrow, settlement,
trust verification) delivered as DataPart payloads — standards-conformant,
payments-aware.

pip install sardis-a2a

Exports: A2AClient, SardisAgentCard, A2APaymentRequest/Response, AgentDiscoveryService, SSE stream.

Coinbase AgentKit

sardis-agentkit · v0.1.0

Sardis payment actions for Coinbase AgentKit. AgentKit handles on-chain identity;
Sardis handles policy and compliance on top — so a CDP-backed agent has
non-custodial wallets and natural-language spend rules without rebuilding either.

pip install sardis-agentkit

Group 04 · Platform

## Where agents run inside someone else's product.

Not every agent is a standalone script. These adapters plug Sardis into platforms where
non-engineers build agents: Vercel's AI SDK, n8n, Activepieces, ChatGPT custom GPTs.

Vercel AI SDK

@sardis/ai-sdk · v1.1.0

Sardis tools packaged as Vercel AI SDK tool() definitions. Drop into
generateText, streamText, or ToolLoopAgent.
Ships ESM + CJS + browser bundles; type-safe via Zod.

npm i @sardis/ai-sdk

MCP Server

@sardis/mcp-server · v1.1.0

50 Sardis tools over Model Context Protocol. Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, or any
MCP host. Simulated mode lets a new user try Sardis from Claude Desktop without an
account. See canvas 14 for the full tool list.

npx @sardis/mcp-server start

n8n

n8n-nodes-sardis · v1.0.0

Community node + credential. Drop Sardis actions (pay, hold, approve, balance, policy
check) into any n8n workflow. Credential type stores API keys in n8n's encrypted store.

npm i -g n8n-nodes-sardis

Activepieces

@sardis/activepieces · v1.0.0

Sardis piece for Activepieces workflow platform. Policy-controlled payments as a
drag-and-drop block for non-engineer builders.

npm i @sardis/activepieces

ChatGPT Custom GPT

sardis-gpt

OpenAPI actions spec + GPT instructions. Gives a ChatGPT custom GPT the ability to
pay, check balance, and enforce policies through Sardis — no backend code required
beyond hosting the action endpoint (which Sardis provides).

Artifacts: openapi-actions.yaml, instructions.md.

OpenAI function-calling

sardis-openai · v1.0.0

Classic OpenAI function-calling wrapper. Complements sardis-openai-agents
for teams still on the bare Chat Completions API.

pip install sardis-openai

Adapter Anatomy

## Every adapter exposes the same five primitives.

The surface area is deliberately tiny. If an agent can do these five things safely, every
meaningful agent-payment use case falls out: order an API call, pay a vendor, refill a
balance, split a cost between two agents, tighten a policy mid-run.

Primitive
Server op
Typical agent use

pay(to, amount, purpose)
POST /v1/payments via PaymentOrchestrator
Any outbound payment — API, vendor, user refund.

check_balance(wallet_id)
GET /v1/wallets/{id}/balance
Decide whether to spend.

set_policy(text | structured)
POST /v1/policies/parse + apply
Self-limit before a risky sub-task.

check_policy(intent)
POST /v1/policies/check
Dry-run: would this pay succeed?

list_transactions(wallet_id)
GET /v1/transactions
Agent retrospection / reporting.

Observability

## Where integration usage shows up in the dashboard.

Every tool invocation tags its payment with a framework hint (SDK-level
X-Sardis-Framework header). The dashboard buckets traffic per framework so
operators see where their spend is actually coming from.

Dashboard page
What it shows

apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/(overview)/page.tsx
Top-line per-framework spend, last 24h/7d/30d.

apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/analytics/page.tsx
Per-framework cost breakdown + transaction counts.

apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/agents/page.tsx
Per-agent framework tag + last-active timestamp.

apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/policy-manager/page.tsx
Policy hits grouped by framework — useful for tuning.

apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/ledger/page.tsx
Raw ledger entries with framework column.

Release

## How integrations ship.

Python integrations go via .github/workflows/release-python-integrations.yml
(tag pattern py-<integration>-v*). JS integrations go via
release-npm.yml (tags: ai-sdk-v*, mcp-v*, etc.).
Each tag cuts a version-stable release; prerelease toggles go to TestPyPI / npm dist-tag.

Adapter
Registry
Release tag prefix

sardis-langchain
PyPI
py-langchain-v*

sardis-crewai
PyPI
py-crewai-v*

sardis-autogpt
PyPI
py-autogpt-v*

sardis-openai / openai-agents
PyPI
py-openai-v* / py-openai-agents-v*

sardis-agent-sdk
PyPI
py-agent-sdk-v*

sardis-adk / a2a
PyPI
py-adk-v* / py-a2a-v*

sardis-agentkit / browser-use
PyPI
py-agentkit-v* / py-browser-use-v*

sardis-composio / openclaw
PyPI
py-composio-v* / py-openclaw-v*

@sardis/ai-sdk
npm
ai-sdk-v*

@sardis/mcp-server
npm
mcp-v*

@sardis/activepieces
npm
activepieces-v*

n8n-nodes-sardis
npm
n8n-v*

sardis-stagehand
npm
stagehand-v*

sardis-e2b
E2B template registry
—

sardis-gpt
ChatGPT actions (hosted)
—

Source of Truth

## Where to verify the claims on this page.

Question
File

Which integrations exist today?
ls packages/ | grep -E 'langchain|crewai|autogpt|openai|agent-sdk|adk|a2a|agentkit|browser-use|composio|openclaw|stagehand|ai-sdk|activepieces|mcp-server|gpt|e2b|n8n'

What does the LangChain toolkit expose?
packages/sardis-langchain/src/sardis_langchain/__init__.py

What tools does the Claude adapter ship?
packages/sardis-agent-sdk/src/sardis_agent_sdk/__init__.py

A2A wire protocol?
packages/sardis-a2a/src/sardis_a2a/

Python integration release flow?
.github/workflows/release-python-integrations.yml

npm integration release flow?
.github/workflows/release-npm.yml

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

14 · Product

SDKs & CLIs

What every adapter is a wrapper around.

13 · Product

Protocol Gateway

AP2 + TAP + x402 + A2A — the standards story.

16 · Product

Dashboard UI

Per-framework analytics and policy tuning.

08 · Flows

Policy Engine

The thing every adapter gates against.


=====================================
# DASHBOARD
https://canvas.sardis.sh/dashboard
=====================================

Canvas 16 · Product

## Dashboard UI

Forty-three operator pages under apps/dashboard/. Next.js 16 App Router (React 19),
Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, monokrom grayscale theme. Built for finance and engineering
operators to see, approve, and kill agent spend without a support ticket — and nothing
on the page competes with the data.

TL;DR
The dashboard is the control plane. It does not sign transactions and it does not
hold private keys. It reads from the same Postgres the API writes to, streams live events
over SSE, and writes back via the same REST surface that SDKs use — with a human in front
of every action.

Stack

## What's running under apps/dashboard/.

Every dependency here is in apps/dashboard/package.json. No component is
custom-rolled when shadcn already has one; no icon set outside Phosphor; no gradients;
no emoji.

Concern
Choice
Why

Framework
Next.js 16.2.1 (App Router) on React 19.2.4
Server Components for the heavy list/detail pages; client islands for live widgets.

Styling
Tailwind v4 + tw-animate-css + tailwind-merge
Monokrom grayscale tokens, no custom CSS file per page.

Component library
shadcn/ui (shadcn@4) + Radix primitives
Owned locally, a11y baked in, no runtime framework.

Icons
@phosphor-icons/react
Monoline, weight-consistent; zero emoji.

Auth
better-auth v1.5 (EdDSA/JWKS) + legacy FastAPI JWT (HS256) dual path
Passkeys, org roles, and a migration path. Plugins: @better-auth/agent-auth, @better-auth/api-key, @better-auth/passkey, @better-auth/stripe.

Billing
@polar-sh/better-auth + @polar-sh/sdk + stripe
Polar now, Stripe-ready when Atlas entity clears.

Analytics
posthog-js
Funnel + retention on the dashboard itself.

Onboarding tour
driver.js v1.4
Six-step dark-themed tour on first login.

Charts
recharts v3
Grayscale palettes, tabular numerals.

Forms
react-hook-form + @hookform/resolvers + input-otp
Schema-validated on client and server.

Toasts
sonner
Low-chrome notifications.

Date
date-fns v4 + react-day-picker v9
Timezone-safe rollups and range pickers.

Observability
@sentry/nextjs v10
Client + server error + session replay.

Captcha
@captchafox/react, @marsidev/react-turnstile
Abuse-prevention on sign-up and reset flows.

Support
@intercom/messenger-js-sdk
In-app conversations for on-call customers.

Fonts
Geist Sans + Geist Mono (self-hosted)
Linear/Vercel-grade legibility without a license bill.

Route Structure

## Two kinds of surface: the protected operator app and public flows.

(dashboard) is a Next.js route group — it applies a shared layout (auth
guard + sidebar + top bar) without appearing in the URL. /demo,
/auth/*, and /account/* live outside the group so they render
without the operator chrome.

[diagram]

flowchart TB
subgraph public[Public / unauth]
DEMO["/demo"]
AUTH["/auth/[path]"]
ACCT["/account/[path]"]
end

subgraph dash["(dashboard) group · auth-gated"]
OV["(overview)/"]
AG["agents · agents/new · wallets · analytics"]
PM["mandates · policy-manager · approvals · approval-config · guardrails · counterparties · fallback-rules"]
SC["kill-switch · evidence · audit-anchors · anomaly-detection · alerts · exceptions"]
PO["transactions · holds · settlements · reconciliation · invoices · mpp-sessions"]
ME["merchants · merchants/analytics · checkout-controls"]
DV["api-keys · api-playground · webhooks · live-events · simulation · workflows"]
IN["observability · provider-health · go-live · environments · virtual-cards · billing"]
SU["support · control-center · settings"]
end

U["User"] --> AUTH --> dash
U --> DEMO

[/diagram]

Group 01 · Overview & Agents

## Who the agents are and what they're doing.

Route
Purpose
Key components
API routers

(dashboard)/(overview)
Org-wide snapshot — active agents, today's spend, last 10 decisions.
StatCards, SSE event feed, recharts sparkline
me, wallets, transactions, live_events

agents
List + filter agents by framework, wallet, last active.
Command palette filter, virtualized table
agents, groups

agents/new
Create agent wizard — pick framework, wallet policy, compliance posture.
Stepper, react-hook-form
agents, policies, kyc_onboarding

wallets
All wallets, balances, chain breakdown, per-wallet mini-policy.
Table with inline drawer detail
wallets, funding, fx

analytics
Usage + spend analytics, per-framework breakdown.
recharts bar/line/area, date-range picker
usage, spending, transactions

Group 02 · Policies & Mandates

## What agents are allowed to do.

Route
Purpose
Key components
API routers

policy-manager
Browse, edit, version natural-language & structured policies.
Diff viewer, policy parser preview
policies, spending_policy

mandates
Active agent-payment mandates (AP2 / A2A delegation).
Mandate chain viewer, signature verifier
mandates, ap2, mandate_delegation

approvals
Pending human-in-the-loop approval queue.
Inbox UI with keyboard nav
approvals, approval_service

approval-config
Which conditions route to humans (amount, counterparty, risk).
Rule builder
approvals, policies

guardrails
Agent-level guardrails (rate, token, purpose).
Toggle matrix
guardrails

counterparties
Allow/block lists, per-counterparty policy.
Searchable table + quick-add
counterparties

fallback-rules
What happens on policy failure — deny, approve, escalate.
Decision tree editor
policies, approvals

Group 03 · Security & Compliance

## The red buttons and the audit trail.

Route
Purpose
Key components
API routers

kill-switch
Org-wide or per-agent pause. Primary red-button UI.
Confirm dialog + countdown
kill_switch, emergency, admin

evidence
Export cryptographic evidence bundles for regulators.
Bundle builder, Merkle proof viewer
evidence, data_export

audit-anchors
On-chain anchor status (SardisLedgerAnchor.sol tx feed).
Tx explorer deeplinks
ledger, anchors

anomaly-detection
ML anomaly scores per agent/wallet/day.
Heatmap, threshold slider
anomaly_detection

alerts
Active alerts, ack/resolve, owner assignment.
Incident board
alerts, monitoring

exceptions
Policy-denied + errored operations, with replay.
Detail drawer + replay button
exceptions

Group 04 · Payments & Ops

## Where real-world money movement surfaces.

Route
Purpose
Key components
API routers

transactions
All tx, filterable by wallet/agent/status/chain.
Virtualized table + detail drawer with tx hash links
transactions, payments

holds
Active + released holds (auth / capture / void).
Action bar: extend, capture, void
holds

settlements
Settlement batches — CCTP burn/mint, fiat ACH/SEPA/PIX.
Batch timeline
settlement, bridge

reconciliation
Reconciliation queue items + manual resolve.
Diff viewer (expected vs. observed)
reconciliation, exceptions

invoices
Org invoices + per-agent line items.
PDF preview, Stripe/Polar links
billing, invoices

mpp-sessions
Stripe MPP sessions (Tempo / mainnet).
Session detail + protocol log
mpp, stripe_connect

merchant, merchant_checkout

Per-merchant volume, conversion, refund rate.
Cohort table + recharts area
merchant, checkout

api_keys, auth

Request builder + JSON diff
All

simulation

simulation

workflows

Block list, trigger preview

observability
Sentry bridge, trace links, request-rate panels.
iframe + trace deep links
monitoring

External provider heartbeats (Turnkey, Didit, Elliptic, Alchemy, Circle).
Status grid with last-check timestamps
health

go-live
Go-live checklist: KYC, KYB, webhook signing, rate limits, kill switch test.
Stepper with inline validation
admin, kyc_onboarding

environments
Manage live/test/sandbox envs, per-env keys, toggles.
Env switcher
environments

virtual-cards
Stripe Issuing card controls — issue, freeze, cancel.
Card detail drawer, spend caps
cards

billing
Plan, seats, usage meter, invoices, payment method (Polar).
Plan matrix + Polar session redirect

Group 08 · Support & Admin

## Escape hatches.

Route
Purpose
Key components
API routers

support
In-app Intercom + quick docs + replay-capable ticket creator.
Intercom widget, replay attach
support

control-center
Platform-admin console — cross-tenant ops (internal).
Role-gated
admin, emergency

settings
Org settings — team, roles, branding, retention.
Sectioned form
me, org

Group 09 · Public flows

## Surfaces that render without the operator chrome.

Route
Purpose

/demo
Unauthenticated demo — try Sardis (sandbox) without signing up.

/auth/[path]
better-auth catch-all: sign-in, sign-up, forgot, reset, verify, passkey.

/account/[path]
Account management after login — profile, sessions, security.

Design Language

## Monokrom, tabular, keyboard-first.

Everything grayscale except rare risk-signal accents (policy-denied red on action; never
on decoration). Phosphor regular weight, 16/20px. Numerals tabular; sparse grid. A page
like (overview)/page.tsx is a stack of shadcn Card blocks — no
dashboards-inside-dashboards, no widget marketplace.

Themes

next-themes drives light/dark; both share information density and
contrast. No third "brand" theme. Toggle lives in the user menu.

Charts

recharts with a single grayscale palette. No pie charts. No background
gradients. Axis labels in Geist Mono; tabular nums for values.

Keyboard

cmdk command palette (⌘K) jumps anywhere; every table supports arrow-key
row navigation; destructive actions always require confirm-with-type.

Icons & forbidden lists

Phosphor only. No emoji anywhere in-product. No gradients. No drop shadows deeper than
shadcn's default. No custom one-off components when a primitive already exists.

Onboarding

## Six-step driver.js tour.

Runs once on first login (localStorage flag sardis-tour-seen).
Dark-themed to match the default dashboard theme. Skippable; restartable from
settings.

#
Anchor
Teaches

1
Sidebar
Core / Policies / Security / Settings organisation.

2
Overview stats
Where today's spend + active-agent count live.

3
agents/new CTA
Two-minute path to a working agent.

4
Policy-manager tile
Natural-language → structured policy preview.

5
Kill-switch tile
The red button — how to pause everything.

6
API keys
Where to mint a sk_test_ key and start building.

Auth

## better-auth + legacy JWT, same session.

better-auth handles the dashboard side (sessions, passkeys, roles, API-key plugin,
Polar billing plugin, agent-auth plugin). A legacy HS256 JWT bridge exists for the
FastAPI side — the server accepts both and emits a principal to the orchestrator.
Passkeys land via @better-auth/passkey.

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant B as Browser
participant NA as Next.js /auth/[path]
participant BA as better-auth (EdDSA/JWKS)
participant NP as Neon (ba_* tables)
participant API as Sardis API (FastAPI)
participant JWT as Legacy JWT (HS256)

B->>NA: POST /auth/sign-in (email+passkey)
NA->>BA: authenticate
BA->>NP: read / write session
BA-->>NA: session cookie (EdDSA)
NA-->>B: 302 /(dashboard)

B->>API: request with session cookie
API->>BA: verify via JWKS
API->>JWT: (fallback) verify HS256 for SDK clients
API-->>B: response (principal attached)

[/diagram]

Source of Truth

## Files to verify against.

Question
File

What pages exist?
find apps/dashboard/app -name page.tsx

Deps / versions?
apps/dashboard/package.json

Overview layout?
apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/(overview)/page.tsx

Policy UI?
apps/dashboard/app/(dashboard)/policy-manager/page.tsx

Auth wiring?
apps/dashboard/app/auth/[path]/page.tsx + lib/auth.ts

Theme tokens?
apps/dashboard/app/globals.css

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

04 · Backend

API Surface

The 123 routers these pages call.

08 · Flows

Policy Engine

What policy-manager is a front-end for.

19 · Ops

Security Model

Auth, principals, and the kill-switch trust boundary.

18 · Ops
