=====================================
# OSP INTEGRATION
https://canvas.sardis.sh/osp-integration
=====================================

Canvas 21 · Extended

## OSP Integration

Open Service Protocol (OSP) is an open standard — separate from Sardis — for AI agents to
discover, provision, rotate, and deprovision developer services. Sardis is a reference payment rail
inside OSP, declared as sardis_wallet in any provider's accepted_payment_methods.
This canvas shows the bridge: what OSP is, why Sardis is one of its rails, and the code that wires them.

TL;DR
OSP is to service provisioning what MCP is to tool access. A provider publishes
/.well-known/osp.json; an agent calls POST /osp/v1/provision; credentials come back
Ed25519-encrypted. OSP is payment-rail agnostic — Sardis plugs in via
@sardis/osp-integration, providing the wallet, escrow, ledger, and an MCP server that exposes
nine OSP tools to Claude / Cursor / other MCP clients.

Motivation

## Why OSP exists.

Every managed service today has its own proprietary onboarding flow. Agents can't fill signup forms,
can't solve CAPTCHAs, can't verify emails. The alternatives are invite-only ecosystems
(Stripe Projects, March 2026) or custom-per-provider glue. OSP is the open-standard third path.

Problem 1

Manual signup is the wrong primitive

Browsers, CAPTCHAs, email verification — interaction patterns agents cannot automate without
brittle scraping. Every new service is bespoke integration work.

Problem 2

Platform lock-in beats open coordination

Stripe Projects is invite-only, Stripe-rail-only. OSP is Apache 2.0; any provider can implement it,
any agent can consume it, any rail can settle it.

Problem 3

Reusable credentials don't fit agent threat models

OSP's core thesis: "Reusable credentials are the wrong primitive. Every service interaction
should be a first-class protocol operation — discoverable, provisionable, rotatable, auditable."

Problem 4

No standard place to settle a bill

Agents can reason about a service spec but have no universal way to pay for it. OSP declares
accepted_payment_methods; Sardis is one of them alongside x402, MPP, Stripe SPT, free.

Protocol Flow

## Discover → provision → rotate → deprovision.

Four operations define the OSP lifecycle. Every provider exposes them under the same URL scheme;
every agent speaks the same JSON. Spec lives at ~/osp/spec/osp-v1.0.md (v1.1 draft).

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent
participant P as Provider (e.g. Supabase)
participant S as Sardis (payment rail)

A->>P: GET /.well-known/osp.json
P-->>A: manifest { offerings, tiers, accepted_payment_methods }
A->>A: Pick offering + tier + payment_method="sardis_wallet"

Note over A,S: Escrow leg (optional but recommended)
A->>S: EscrowManager.hold(amount, release_condition)
S-->>A: escrow_id + signed proof

A->>P: POST /osp/v1/provision { offering_id, tier_id, agent_public_key, payment_proof: escrow_id }
P->>S: verify escrow_id
S-->>P: held=true, amount ok
P->>P: allocate resource_id
P-->>A: { resource_id, credentials (Ed25519-encrypted) }

Note over A,S: Settlement leg
P->>S: release(escrow_id) on successful provision
S->>S: ledger append (credit provider, debit agent mandate)

opt Rotation
A->>P: POST /osp/v1/rotate/{resource_id}
P-->>A: new encrypted credentials
end

opt Deprovision
A->>P: POST /osp/v1/deprovision/{resource_id}
P-->>A: receipt
end

[/diagram]

The Bridge

## Where Sardis fits in an OSP flow.

OSP's provision-request.schema.json enumerates seven payment methods:
free, sardis_wallet, stripe_spt, x402,
mpp, invoice, external. Sardis is the first-class crypto-native rail.

// Provider manifest — /.well-known/osp.json
{
"provider_id": "supabase.com",
"offerings": [{
"offering_id": "supabase/postgres",
"tiers": [
{ "tier_id": "free", "price": { "amount": "0", "currency": "USD" } },
{ "tier_id": "pro", "price": { "amount": "25", "currency": "USD", "interval": "P1M" } }
]
}],
"accepted_payment_methods": ["free", "sardis_wallet", "stripe_spt", "x402"]
}

When the agent sends payment_method: "sardis_wallet", the payment_proof is a Sardis
escrow ID or charge intent. The provider verifies it out-of-band with the Sardis API
(/api/v2/escrow/{id}) before allocating the resource. On successful provisioning the escrow
releases; on failure the hold is refunded.

The Package

## @sardis/osp-integration — three modules.

Lives at ~/osp/sardis-integration/ (peer to the OSP core, shipped as its own npm
package). Three modules, each a clean seam.

Module · payment/

Wallet · escrow · ledger · charge

SardisWalletClient, EscrowManager, Ledger, and the typed
shapes for SpendingMandate, EscrowHold, ChargeIntent,
LedgerEntry. The minimum surface a provider needs to accept sardis_wallet.

Module · mcp/

Nine OSP tools for MCP clients

sardis-mcp-server.ts exposes: discover_services,
provision_service, deprovision, rotate_credentials,
check_status, get_usage, estimate_cost,
list_skills, env_generate. Makes Claude/Cursor first-class OSP consumers.

Module · cli/

Sardis CLI extension

commands.ts + bridge.ts. Adds sardis osp discover,
sardis osp provision, etc. — same bridge as the MCP server, command-line shape. The
payment layer is shared.

Shared Cryptography

## Ed25519 everywhere.

Both OSP and Sardis settled on Ed25519 for identity + envelope encryption. This is not coincidence —
it means a single agent keypair can simultaneously sign a Sardis mandate, prove identity to an OSP
provider, and decrypt the returned credential bundle.

Use case
Primitive
Where it lives

Agent identity (KYA/TAP)
Ed25519 signature over canonical JSON
Sardis sardis-protocol (TAP verifier)

Spending mandate signature
Ed25519 signature over mandate hash
Sardis spending_mandate.py

OSP provision request signature
Ed25519 signature over canonical request
OSP osp-crypto crate

OSP credential envelope
x25519 key agreement → XChaCha20-Poly1305
OSP credential-bundle.schema.json

On-chain ledger anchor
Secp256k1 tx sig, Merkle root payload
Sardis SardisLedgerAnchor.sol

Strategic Framing

## Two open standards, one author, one platform thesis.

Sardis and OSP are deliberately separate projects. Sardis is a product (fintech rail, VC-fundable).
OSP is a standard (Apache 2.0, zero revenue target). They reinforce each other because every OSP
provider that accepts sardis_wallet puts Sardis in front of another agent cohort — and
every Sardis agent that wants to provision a DB, a logger, a feature flag service gets a clean OSP
path to do so.

Sardis — the product layer

Non-custodial wallet, MPC signing, spending mandates, KYC/AML, on-chain anchor, 15+ framework
SDKs. Revenue = platform fee + enterprise compliance tier. Live on Base + Tempo mainnet.

OSP — the standards layer

Protocol spec (9,600+ lines, v1.1 draft). Apache 2.0. SDKs in Rust, TypeScript, Python, Go.
Rebranded public umbrella: Pact. Maximum adoption, zero revenue ambition — the
moat is in being the default spec that Sardis (and others) implement.

Status · April 2026

## Where each side actually is.

Surface
State
Location

OSP spec
v1.1 draft · ~9,600 lines · active development
~/osp/spec/osp-v1.0.md

OSP JSON schemas
10 schemas (draft 2020-12) + 14 example manifests
~/osp/schemas/

OSP core (Rust)
Crates for crypto, manifest, vault, CLI (18 cmds), registry, provider, conformance, SDK
~/osp/osp-core/crates/

TypeScript SDK
v0.2.0 · 139 tests
~/osp/reference-implementation/typescript/

Python SDK
v0.2.0 · 171 tests
~/osp/reference-implementation/python/

Go SDK
Active
~/osp/osp-sdk-go/

Sardis ↔ OSP bridge
@sardis/osp-integration — payment + MCP + CLI modules
~/osp/sardis-integration/src/

Sardis MPC-server-for-OSP
9 tools · MCP-compatible (Claude Desktop, Cursor)
~/osp/sardis-integration/src/mcp/sardis-mcp-server.ts

Website
Live — osp.sh
~/osp/website/

Source of Truth

## Key files.

File
What's in it

~/osp/README.md
Pitch, protocol summary, v1.1 feature list, repo layout.

~/osp/spec/osp-v1.0.md
Full normative spec (v1.1 draft).

~/osp/schemas/service-manifest.schema.json
The /.well-known/osp.json shape.

~/osp/schemas/provision-request.schema.json
Defines payment_method enum including sardis_wallet.

~/osp/schemas/credential-bundle.schema.json
Ed25519-encrypted credential envelope format.

~/osp/sardis-integration/src/index.ts
Barrel export: payment + mcp + cli modules.

~/osp/sardis-integration/src/payment/index.ts
SardisWalletClient, EscrowManager, Ledger.

~/osp/sardis-integration/src/payment/types.ts
SpendingMandate, EscrowHold, ChargeIntent, LedgerEntry.

~/osp/sardis-integration/src/mcp/sardis-mcp-server.ts
Nine MCP tools: discover / provision / deprovision / rotate / check / usage / estimate / skills / env.

~/osp/sardis-integration/src/cli/commands.ts
Sardis CLI subcommands for OSP lifecycle.

~/osp/reference-implementation/
TS + Python reference SDKs (v0.2.0).

## Follow-ups.

20 · Extended

Sardis Connect

The inverse wedge: turn any HTTP API into a sardis_wallet-accepting endpoint in three lines.

13 · Product

Protocol Gateway

AP2, TAP, x402, A2A — the other protocols Sardis verifies. OSP sits alongside, focused on provisioning rather than payment.

08 · Flows

Policy Engine

The mandate engine that backs every sardis_wallet payment_proof in an OSP provision request.

=====================================
# ZK PRIVACY
https://canvas.sardis.sh/zk-privacy
=====================================

Canvas 22 · Extended

## ZK Privacy Stack

Prove a payment satisfied its policy, mandate, funding, and identity requirements —
without revealing the amounts, the limits, the merchant, or the agent. Sardis ships two
Noir circuit packages (sardis-zk-policy, sardis-zkp), a deterministic
Python simulator for CI, and three adjacent runtime layers — sardis-ucp,
sardis-guardrails, and the PreExecutionPipeline — that decide when
a clear-text decision needs to turn into a proof.

TL;DR
Enterprises can't publish raw spending logs to auditors — the logs are the product.
ZK lets the same row say "this payment was within all 11 policy rules" without showing
per-transaction amounts, merchant identities, or the limits that define the rules.
Sardis uses Noir + Barretenberg (Aztec) for the circuits, because the
toolchain is audited, the proving backend is fast enough for per-transaction use, and
verification is cheap enough to run on-chain via SardisLedgerAnchor.

Status

## What ships vs. what's experimental.

The ZK stack is real code — two Noir projects, working circuits, a Python simulator used in CI.
It is not on the default hot path. Today it runs as an alternative policy check for
tenants that explicitly opt in; the clear-text PreExecutionPipeline remains the
production default.

Component
Maturity
Deployed
Gate

sardis-zk-policy (Noir circuit + simulator)
Feature-complete for 5 / 11 policy checks
No (opt-in)
Requires nargo installed in the runtime image.

sardis-zkp (mandate / identity / funding circuits)
Circuits compile, dev-mode mock proofs only
No
production=True on ZKProver — refuses to mock-verify otherwise.

sardis-ucp (Universal Commerce Protocol)
Shipped
Yes
Public surface; feeds checkout + AP2 adapters.

sardis-guardrails (circuit breaker, kill switch, limiter, ML fraud)
Shipped
Yes
Always on. Not ZK — classical runtime safety layer.

Backend Choice

## Why Noir (Aztec) — and not Circom / Halo2 / RISC0.

Rust-like syntax, small surface

Circuit authors are Python and Rust engineers, not cryptographers. Noir reads like Rust —
assert, for, if, u64 — which keeps the
policy circuit reviewable alongside the clear-text Python implementation in
policy_circuit_sim.py.

ACIR → ACVM → Barretenberg

Noir compiles to ACIR (intermediate representation), executed by the
ACVM witness solver, and proven by Barretenberg (UltraPlonk
+ Honk). That layering means we can swap proving backends later without rewriting circuits.

Audited toolchain

Aztec audits its compiler and prover. The alternative — hand-rolled Circom — requires writing
templates + solvers + verifiers that all need independent review. For a fintech, using an
audited toolchain is cheaper than auditing custom R1CS.

Cheap on-chain verification

Barretenberg emits Solidity verifier contracts. One ZK policy proof verifies in ~300K gas on
Base L2 — low enough to anchor alongside the Merkle batch from SardisLedgerAnchor.sol
without blowing the per-batch cost model.

Package · sardis-zk-policy

## Policy circuit — 5 of 11 checks in zero knowledge.

A single Noir binary at packages/sardis-zk-policy/src/main.nr. Private inputs
carry the transaction + policy parameters; a single public input pair
(evidence_hash_high, evidence_hash_low) links the proof to the
off-chain policy decision log.

main.nr — policy compliance proof

packages/sardis-zk-policy/src/main.nr · Nargo.toml · Prover.toml · Verifier.toml

Proves all five checks in one circuit: per-tx cap, daily cap, MCC block-list membership,
time-window (including wrap-around), and per-merchant cap. Asserts evidence_hash
is non-zero so the off-chain row is addressable.

Private inputs (witness)

amount: u64

daily_total: u64

merchant_total: u64

mcc_hash: u64

hour: u8

max_per_transaction: u64

daily_limit: u64

per_merchant_limit: u64

window_start: u8

window_end: u8

blocked_mcc_hashes: [u64; 8]

Public inputs

evidence_hash_high: pub u128

evidence_hash_low: pub u128

policy_circuit_sim.py — bit-exact Python mirror

packages/sardis-zk-policy/python/policy_circuit_sim.py · types.py

A Python function that runs the same five checks in the same order, producing a
CircuitResult(passed, failed_check). Used in CI for fast regression tests; the
companion suite tests/test_noir_equivalence.py is designed to pin Noir output
to the Python output once nargo prove is wired into CI.

API

simulate_circuit(private, public)

prepare_circuit_inputs(...)

mcc_to_hash(mcc_code)

evidence_hash_to_pair(hex)

Guarantees

Same assert order as main.nr

Same wrap-around time logic

Same MCC zero-slot handling

Deterministic, no I/O

Package · sardis-zkp

## Reusable circuit library — mandate, identity, funding.

Three Noir circuits under packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/, orchestrated by a Python
ZKProver in src/lib.py. Each circuit targets one question; together
they cover the questions Sardis actually needs to answer in ZK at the mandate layer.

mandate_compliance.nr — payment within mandate bounds

packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/mandate_compliance.nr

Proves amount ≤ per_tx_limit AND daily_spent + amount ≤ daily_limit,
and that the private inputs hash to the public mandate_commitment and
payment_commitment (both Poseidon on BN254). Merchant inclusion via 8-deep
Merkle proof; replay protection via mandate_nonce.

Public

mandate_commitment: Field

payment_commitment: Field

Private

amount, per_tx_limit,

daily_limit, daily_spent,

merchant_id_hash,

allowed_merchant_root,

merchant_proof[8],

mandate_nonce

identity_proof.nr — KYA level without agent identity

packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/identity_proof.nr

Proves the agent holds an attestation whose kya_level ≥ required_kya_level,
unexpired, and not future-dated. Public side sees only the commitment + the threshold;
the agent's id hash and the issuer remain private.

Public

identity_commitment: Field

required_kya_level: Field

Private

agent_id_hash, kya_level,

attestation_timestamp,

attestation_expiry,

current_timestamp,

issuer_id_hash,

attestation_nonce

funding_sufficiency.nr — cells cover amount

packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/funding_sufficiency.nr

Proves sum(cell_values[0..16]) ≥ payment_amount without revealing any individual
cell value. Each cell is bounded to < 253 to prevent field-overflow attacks.
Commitments are Poseidon batches so the off-chain funding-cell set can be reconciled.

Public

payment_amount: Field

cells_commitment: Field

Private

cell_values[16], cell_ids[16],

cell_count, currency_hash,

nonce

Privacy Tiers

## Transparent, hybrid, full ZK.

Defined in sardis_zkp.PrivacyTier. Tenants select a tier per payment object
(mandate, payment, wallet) — the orchestrator routes to the corresponding verifier path.

Tier
What's visible
What's hidden
When to use

TRANSPARENT
All fields (amount, merchant, limits, counterparty)
Nothing
Default. Consumer agents, developer sandboxes, public tokens.

HYBRID
Merchant, chain, timestamp, decision outcome
Amount, limits
B2B payments where merchant identity is non-sensitive but commercial terms are.

FULL_ZK
Proof only — commitments + verification result
Amount, merchant, limits, agent identity, funding cells
Enterprise treasury flows, regulated payouts, custodial agent fleets.

Coverage Matrix

## Which policy checks translate to ZK — and which don't.

The SpendingPolicy engine has ~11 check types. Five are implemented in
main.nr today. The rest either need oracle data, a running graph, or third-party
attestations that can't be proven in-circuit without leaking the external state.

Policy check
ZK today
ZK feasible
Why / why not

Per-transaction cap
Yes
—
Pure numeric comparison.

Daily aggregate cap
Yes
—
Running total carried as private input.

Per-merchant cap
Yes
—
Keyed by merchant_id_hash; same shape as daily.

MCC block-list
Yes (8 slots)
—
Set-membership via linear scan; extend to Merkle for larger lists.

Time window (with wrap)
Yes
—
Hour as u8; handled in both linear and wrap case.

Allowed-merchant Merkle root
Partial (in sardis-zkp mandate)
Yes
Need to wire merchant registry → Merkle root into prover input.

KYC/AML status
No
Yes, via attested credential
Requires a KYC-provider-signed attestation usable inside circuit.

Sanctions / OFAC screen
No
Partial
Elliptic API is external; proof would cover attestation, not live screen.

Anomaly score threshold
No
Partial
Score is an ML output; circuit could assert a signed score, not compute it.

Rate limit (token bucket)
No
Yes, with state commitment
Needs a commitment to bucket state; opens replay questions.

Kill switch / emergency stop
No
No
Kill switch is operator intent, not a math fact. Stays clear-text.

Flow

## Prover → verifier, end to end.

A ZK-tier payment takes the same orchestrator spine as a transparent one — it just forks at
the policy check into a proof-generation path instead of a direct assert.

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent
participant O as PaymentOrchestrator
participant P as PreExecutionPipeline
participant ZP as ZKProver (sardis-zkp)
participant N as Noir / nargo
participant L as Ledger + Anchor
participant V as On-chain Verifier

A->>O: execute(intent, privacy_tier=FULL_ZK)
O->>P: run classical gates (rate limit, kill switch)
P->>ZP: prove_mandate_compliance(amount, limits)
ZP->>N: write Prover.toml + nargo prove
N-->>ZP: proof bytes + public inputs
ZP->>P: ZKProof(commitments, bytes)
P->>P: hash evidence row → evidence_hash
P->>ZP: prove_policy(evidence_hash, witness)
ZP-->>P: policy proof bytes
P-->>O: approved (ZK)
O->>L: append row + attach proofs
L->>V: submit Merkle root + ZK proof batch
V-->>L: on-chain receipt

[/diagram]

Worked Example

## "Prove spending was under $500 today — without per-tx amounts."

Concrete path through sardis-zk-policy for a single transaction. All amounts
scaled to integer minor-units (1 USDC = 1,000,000).

Prover side (private)

amount
47_500000 (47.50 USDC)

daily_total
392_000000 (already spent today)

max_per_transaction
250_000000

daily_limit
500_000000

hour / window
14 / [09, 18]

mcc_hash
sha256("5812")[:8]

blocked_mcc_hashes
[gambling, crypto_mx, ...]

Public (what verifiers see)

evidence_hash
sha256(decision_row)

→ high / low
two u128 values

Verifier checks
Barretenberg verifier accepts the proof against the compiled circuit and the public inputs.

Auditor sees
"Policy row pol_0x… passed all 5 in-circuit checks." No amounts. No merchant. No limits.

Adjacent · sardis-ucp

## Universal Commerce Protocol — the object model ZK wraps.

UCP is not ZK, but it defines the commerce primitives (UCPCartMandate,
UCPPaymentMandate, UCPOrder, UCPCheckoutCapability) that
ZK proofs reference as commitment inputs. When mandate_compliance.nr takes a
payment_commitment, the preimage is a UCP mandate object.

UCP primitive
File
ZK touch point

UCPPaymentMandate / UCPCartMandate
packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/models/mandates.py
Commitment preimage for mandate_compliance.

UCPCheckoutCapability
packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/capabilities/checkout.py
Session lifecycle — where privacy tier is chosen.

UCPOrder / UCPFulfillment
packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/models/orders.py
Post-settlement state; fulfillment events anchor next to ZK receipts.

ap2.py adapter
packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/adapters/ap2.py
Translates AP2 Intent → Cart → Payment into UCP, so AP2 flows can opt into ZK.

Transports (REST, MCP)
packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/transports/
Declare privacy tier on the wire.

Adjacent · sardis-guardrails

## Classical runtime safety — always in front of the proof.

Guardrails are not ZK; they run first. A proof that a payment satisfied a policy is worthless
if the agent is on the kill list or the circuit breaker is tripped. Guardrails answer
"should we even be computing a proof right now?"

Module
Role
When it fires

circuit_breaker.py
Automatic failure isolation per agent
N consecutive failures → reject without prover work.

kill_switch.py
Global / per-org / per-agent hard stop
Operator or FRAUD/ANOMALY auto-trip.

rate_limiter.py
Token-bucket + sliding window
Too many tx or too much amount per minute/hour.

input_validator.py
Address / amount / chain / token validation
Before any downstream work, including proving.

behavioral_monitor.py + anomaly_engine.py + ml_fraud.py
Statistical / ML anomaly scoring
Generates signed score that a ZK circuit can consume.

graph_fraud.py
Graph-level counterparty analysis
Runs over counterparty network; feeds FIDES trust graph.

agent_threat_detector.py · fingerprint.py · ft3_taxonomy.py
Agent-level threat classification
Feeds KYA attestations used by identity_proof.nr.

zen_engine.py
Rules DSL executor
Declarative merchant / policy rules applied pre-orchestrator.

Integration

## Where ZK hooks into the spine.

The PreExecutionPipeline runs all transactions through the same ordered gate
(see canvas 01 · Architecture). The ZK path is a fork inside the policy gate, not a
parallel pipeline — everything else (mandate check, AP2 verify, rate limit, kill switch) is
shared with the transparent path.

What the orchestrator does differently

On privacy_tier=FULL_ZK, the policy check returns a ZKProof instead
of a boolean. The orchestrator attaches the proof to the ledger row, and the Merkle batch
submission includes proof bytes for on-chain verification.

What stays the same

Kill switch, rate limit, input validation, MPC signing path, CCTP settlement, reconciliation
queue. ZK changes what is seen; it doesn't change what is done.

Dev vs. prod mode

ZKProver(production=False) emits JSON placeholder proof bytes and refuses
to verify. production=True shells out to nargo prove /
nargo verify in the circuit directory. Mock verify was removed on purpose to stop
anyone from mistaking dev mode for security.

On-chain anchor

Verifier contracts generated by Barretenberg are deployed alongside
SardisLedgerAnchor.sol (canvas 06). A batch root + proof tuple posted there is
independently verifiable by any third party without reading Sardis logs.

Source of Truth

## Key files.

File
Purpose

packages/sardis-zk-policy/src/main.nr
The 5-check policy circuit. Binary target (type = "bin" in Nargo.toml).

packages/sardis-zk-policy/Prover.toml
Template for prover-side inputs; populated at runtime.

packages/sardis-zk-policy/Verifier.toml
Public inputs shape — evidence_hash_high, evidence_hash_low.

packages/sardis-zk-policy/python/policy_circuit_sim.py
Python mirror of the circuit. CI uses this for regression.

packages/sardis-zk-policy/python/types.py
PolicyCircuitInputs, PolicyCircuitPublicInputs, hash helpers.

packages/sardis-zk-policy/tests/test_circuit_sim.py
Correctness tests for the Python simulator.

packages/sardis-zk-policy/tests/test_noir_equivalence.py
Pin Noir output to Python simulator output (when nargo is on CI).

packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/mandate_compliance.nr
Mandate per-tx + daily bounds + commitment check.

packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/identity_proof.nr
KYA-level proof without revealing agent id.

packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/funding_sufficiency.nr
Sum of 16 cells ≥ payment amount.

packages/sardis-zkp/src/lib.py
ZKProver + PrivacyTier + CircuitType + dev-mode guard.

packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/models/mandates.py
UCP mandate objects — commitment preimages.

packages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/adapters/ap2.py
AP2 ↔ UCP bridge.

packages/sardis-guardrails/src/sardis_guardrails/*.py
15 runtime-safety modules that gate whether a proof is worth computing.

Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

08 · Flows

Policy Engine

The 11 clear-text policy checks. ZK translates five of them.

06 · Backend

Smart Contracts

SardisLedgerAnchor — where Merkle batches and ZK proofs get posted.

13 · Product

Protocol Gateway

AP2 mandate chains — the preimage for payment_commitment.

19 · Ops

Security Model

Where ZK fits in the fail-closed threat model.
